text.title: Financial Basics — Agent Rules
text.summary: Safety rules for agents generating P&L, computing unit economics, and categorizing transactions.

rule.statement: All currency arithmetic must use Python Decimal or an equivalent fixed-precision type. Never use floating-point for financial calculations; rounding errors in natural-language reasoning compound across aggregations.
rule.rationale: Floating-point arithmetic produces errors like $0.01 discrepancies that compound in monthly summaries. Financial calculations require exact decimal arithmetic.

rule.statement: Stripe MRR requires reconciliation against Stripe's own MRR endpoint, not re-computation from payment records. Upgrades, downgrades, coupons, and timing differences produce different numbers. Flag when self-computed MRR diverges from Stripe MRR by more than 1%.
rule.rationale: Stripe's MRR calculation includes subscription-level adjustments that are not visible in raw payment exports. Agent-computed MRR regularly diverges by 3-8% in products with coupons or mid-cycle changes.

rule.statement: Report refunds and chargebacks separately from revenue. Do not net them silently. The chargeback window (30-90 days) means current-month profit is always provisional until the window closes.
rule.rationale: Reporting net revenue before the chargeback window closes overstates current-month profit. For recurring billing products, late chargebacks from canceling customers appear weeks after the revenue was recognized.

rule.statement: When categorizing transactions, bias toward false negatives (flag as personal / unknown) rather than false positives (claim as business). Uncategorized is recoverable; wrongly claimed business expenses are a tax liability.
rule.rationale: Overstating business deductions creates audit risk. It is safer to understate and have the founder correct upward than to overstate and require an amended return.

rule.statement: Transaction-level financial data includes sensitive PII (merchant names, amounts, timing patterns). Use local models or field-level redaction before sending raw data to external LLM APIs. Summary metrics only through Anthropic APIs.
rule.rationale: Transaction history can reveal personal habits, health spending, relationship status, and business secrets. Sending raw transactions to a cloud API is a privacy leak vector even without names or account numbers.
